source_clause_title: 2. Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule, Section 402(c)(3) of the Toxic

entities.name: Lead Safe Work Practices Obligation

properties.description: Require lead safe work practices to reduce exposure to lead hazards created by renovation, repair and painting activities that disturb lead-based paint
properties.applicable_laws: Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule (RRP), title 40 CFR part 745
properties.applicable_locations: homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978
properties.certification_requirement: renovations must be conducted by certified renovation firms using renovators trained by EPA-accredited training providers
properties.area_thresholds: 6 square feet or greater indoors, 20 square feet or greater outdoors

entities.evidenceQuote: The EPA requires lead safe work practices to reduce exposure to lead hazards created by renovation, repair and painting activities that disturb lead-based paint. Pursuant to the Renovation, Repair and Painting Rule (RRP), renovations in homes, childcare facilities, and schools built prior to 1978 must be conducted by certified renovations firms, using renovators with training by a EPA-accredited training provider, and fully and adequately complying with all applicable laws, rules and regulations governing lead-based materials, including those rules and regulations appearing within title 40 of the Code of Federal Regulations as part 745 (40 CFR 745). The RRP requirements apply to all contractors who disturb lead-based paint in a six-square-foot or greater area indoors or a 20-square-foot or greater area outdoors.

entities.name: Certification Exemption Obligation

properties.description: No federal certification required if a DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor determines a home built before 1978 is lead-free

entities.evidenceQuote: If a DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor determines that a home constructed before 1978 is lead-free, the federal certification is not required for anyone working on that particular building.

relationships.evidenceQuote: The EPA requires lead safe work practices to reduce exposure to lead hazards created by renovation, repair and painting activities that disturb lead-based paint.
If a DPH-certified inspector or risk assessor determines that a home constructed before 1978 is lead-free, the federal certification is not required for anyone working on that particular building.
